frame_analyses.description: Overhead view of laparoscopic needle driver approaching Penrose drain with suture loop. Timer shows 00.00 seconds. Needle driver positioned with needle at approximately 2/3 point from tip.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Task appears to be intracorporeal suture placement on Penrose drain. Initial needle positioning observed. Clean instrument control.

frame_analyses.description: Needle approaching drain entry point. Timer: 00.87 seconds. Needle-tip contact with drain slit visible.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Perpendicular needle entry angle being established. Controlled approach.

frame_analyses.description: Needle passing through drain. Timer: 01.84 seconds. Entry and exit points on drain slit visible.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Needle trajectory appears controlled. Suture follows through slit cleanly.

frame_analyses.description: Instruments positioning for first knot throw. Left instrument (grasper/driver) visible at top. Suture slack visible below. Timer: 02.84 seconds.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Transitioning to knot formation phase. Instrument repositioning for hand wrap.

frame_analyses.description: First throw being executed. Suture loop forming around drain. Timer: 03.47 seconds.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Knot formation in progress. Loop appears to be forming with appropriate tension.

frame_analyses.description: Suture configuration showing partially formed loop structure. Timer: 03.47 seconds (frozen frame or slow action).
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Loop configuration visible. Suture placement on drain slit appears centered.

frame_analyses.description: Black screen with text: 'Where there's a will, there's a way.' and 'Pray for world peace.'
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Motivational conclusion. Video ends with philosophical message.

confidence_rationale: This video is primarily instructional/educational content with title screens, transition overlays, and motivational messaging. Only approximately 6 seconds of actual task performance is visible (frames 11–16), which is insufficient for a complete FLS task assessment. The visible portion appears to show intracorporeal suture placement on a Penrose drain, but the video cuts off before knot completion, suture cutting, or task closure. The '3 Seconds 3 Knots' overlay suggests this may be a teaching module emphasizing knot speed rather than a full performance evaluation.

Brief task demonstration segment (estimated 3–4 seconds of actual knot work) shows competent needle control and clean suture passage through drain, but task is incomplete. Content emphasizes speed principle ('3 Seconds 3 Knots') over full performance assessment.
